item.title: GCP provider adapter (Compute Engine + cloud-init)
item.intent: GCP Compute Engine completes big-three cloud coverage. Implement GCPAdapter following the proven provider interface pattern. Users with GCP credits or existing GCP infrastructure can deploy agents with the same single clawhq deploy command.
item.description: Implement GCPAdapter in src/cloud/provisioning/providers/gcp.ts. Uses Google Cloud Compute API for VM lifecycle. Launches e2-micro (free tier) or e2-small. Creates firewall rule on default network. Credentials via gcloud CLI or service account JSON. Same cloud-init bootstrap as DO/AWS.
steps: Implement Compute Engine client: create, delete, get, set metadata
Create cloud-init startup script for Debian/Ubuntu GCE images
Build firewall rule: allow 443 + gateway port from 0.0.0.0/0
Implement machine image snapshot for fast re-provision
Wire into provider interface with credential validation
acceptanceCriteria: GCE instance launched and agent running within 3 minutes
Firewall rule restricts inbound to HTTPS + gateway port
Works with gcloud CLI credentials or service account key
Free tier eligible instance type used by default
